mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong labor market generally correlates with economic growth.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns following periods of positive PAYEMS growth are not statistically significantly positive (p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate consistently below 4.5% signals a tight labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and potential central bank tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as profit margins are squeezed.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically, very low unemployment can signal late-cycle risks.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5% over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales growth combined with stable or declining consumer price inflation indicates strong consumer demand without overheating, fostering an environment conducive to corporate profit growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth < 2.0%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of periods where both conditions are met, or if the Rank IC for this signal is below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy, which typically raises the cost of capital and discounts future earnings more aggressively, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS has increased for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:Monetary policy tightening often correlates with market headwinds.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances during periods of sustained FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong month-over-month growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer confidence and spending, directly boosting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Retail sales are a direct measure of consumer demand.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is negative when RSAFS month-over-month growth exceeds 0.3% over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates a healthy manufacturing sector and increasing supply-side capacity utilization, which supports overall economic growth and translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods where INDPRO growth exceeds 2.0% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained high level of housing starts indicates strong residential investment and construction activity, which has broad positive spillover effects on the economy and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market strength is a key economic indicator.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances when the HOUST 3-month average is above 1100.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high consumer price inflation erodes purchasing power and can lead to tighter monetary policy, increasing uncertainty and potentially compressing corporate profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:High inflation can negatively impact equity valuations.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of periods where CPIAUCSL 12-month change exceeds 3.0%.
